name: Honda Accord (9th Gen)
engine: 2.4L DOHC i-VTEC I4 (K24W) 'Earth Dreams' — 3.5L SOHC i-VTEC V6 (J35Y6) with VCM — 2.0L two-motor Hybrid
transmission: CVT or 6-speed manual (2.4L) · 6-speed automatic (V6 sedan) / 6-speed manual (V6 coupe)
reliability: 9/10 — widely regarded as one of the most reliable used sedans you can buy. The 2.4L 'Earth Dreams' four with the CVT has an excellent record; the documented caveats are the V6's VCM oil consumption and motor-mount wear, and the industry-wide Takata airbag recall (verify completion). This is also the last Accord generation offered with the J35 V6 — see our Accord V6 page for that engine in detail.
cost_to_own: Insurance: Average · Maintenance ~$400/yr · Excellent economy on the I4 and strong resale across the board
popular_mods: VCM disabler on V6 cars (the standard fix for oil burn and motor mounts)
All-weather liners and tasteful wheels with quality tires
Apple CarPlay/Android Auto retrofit on pre-2016 head units
Honest take: the 2.4 CVT is the sensible buy; the V6 coupe 6-speed is the cult pick

generations.9th Gen (2013–2017): Sharper, lighter, and very efficient. 2.4L 'Earth Dreams' four (185–189hp) with a CVT or 6-speed manual, the 278hp J35 V6 (sedan 6-speed auto / coupe 6-speed manual), and a two-motor Hybrid. The last Accord with the naturally-aspirated V6.
generations.2016 refresh: Restyled front and rear, with standard Apple CarPlay and Android Auto added.

common_issues: V6 VCM-related oil consumption and motor-mount wear
Takata airbag-inflator recall (this generation is included) — verify completion by VIN
Early dual-screen infotainment quirks on pre-2016 cars
Mild direct-injection intake-valve carbon on the V6 at high mileage
maintenance_tips: V6 owners commonly fit a VCM disabler; check oil level between changes
Confirm the Takata airbag recall is complete
Service the CVT fluid with genuine Honda HCF-2 on schedule
Iridium spark plugs around 100k on the V6
